title: Dactylorchis salina
intro: Dactylorchis salina, also known as Dactylorhiza salina, is a type of orchid plant commonly found in Europe and Asia, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorchis salina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as respiratory issues, digestive problems, and wounds.
taxonomy: Dactylorchis salina belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, consisting of over 30,000 known species. Within the Orchidaceae family, Dactylorchis salina is classified as a terrestrial orchid. The genus Dactylorchis is now considered a synonym of Neotinea, making Dactylorchis salina a member of the Neotinea genus. The exact classification of Dactylorchis salina may vary depending on the source and any recent taxonomic revisions.
